data.body:
PASS 1 review — T8 (LLM / prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]. Target: the sibling Phase-1 plan "Author 3 eval specs + offline discipline tests (Phase 1)" (completion-verifier / ticket-quality / spec-alignment specs). Recorded here for traceability (this plan relates to the shipped eval-suite seam owned in this ticket's neighborhood). APPLICABLE: the plan authors LLM eval specs with case schemas, an `expect` value vocabulary, registered deterministic scorers, a cross-family judge, and named output contracts, so the overlay engages. Tool-grounded against src/rebar/llm/eval.py, eval_scorers.py, the shipped eval_specs/, and .github/workflows/eval.yml. ONE PROVEN structural gap (an `expect`-enum value the completion-verifier needs but the plan's vocabulary never names); the rest of the probe passes.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 criteria this chunk satisfies — positive feedback, not findings):
- protocol/decision-rule co-located with the schema that needs it: the plan binds each spec's gating discipline to the dataset it scores in one packaged file (generator/epochs/at_least(k)/coverage/scorers/dataset/gold_set), mirroring the shipped specs where the `expect: finding|pass` decision rule sits with the labeled cases (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:16-49) and the discrimination rule sits with the {true,false} pairs (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:14-30,49-94). The decision rule travels with the schema.
- no ambiguous counter/state increment: epochs/at_least(k)/coverage_threshold are single-site per-spec accumulators (the plan says "epochs>=2, at_least(k), coverage threshold"), and the kappa gold-subset mechanic is co-located per spec — no floating or duplicated counter.
- referenced scorers/contracts exist and are fully specified (tool-verified): "every deterministic scorer registered" is enforced by the SHIPPED validator — validate_scorer rejects any deterministic name not in rebar.llm.eval_scorers.REGISTRY when `known` is passed (eval.py:124-132), and validate_eval_spec(strict=True) wires that in (eval.py:301-307); the "judge disciplined + gates:false" rule the plan asserts is exactly what validate_scorer enforces (pinned cross-family grader, temp 0, integer seed, dated snapshot, gates:true rejected — eval.py:133-160). The named seam exists; the plan's discipline claims resolve to real checks.
- fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step specified: the offline-vs-live split degrades safely — AC#5 mandates the eval-discipline CI validate all specs at ZERO API cost (the .github/workflows/eval.yml offline job validates every packaged spec with no credentials), with the live model run fenced behind the [eval] extra. AC#4's per-spec offline unit tests (validity / non-empty gold_set / balance / registered scorers) back-stop the no-model path. (Note: the CI's "validate every packaged eval spec" step calls validate_eval_spec WITHOUT strict=True — eval.yml:50 — so the registered-scorer/balance/gold checks ride on the AC#4 unit tests, not that loop; AC#4 assigns them there explicitly, so this is consistent, not a gap. Worth a confirming sentence in the plan that the unit tests are the strict-mode carrier, but no finding.)
- no instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern: the plan reuses "the schema + registry from the foundation task" rather than re-deriving a parallel vocabulary; no contradictory or distractor instruction.

FINDING (T8 — an `expect`-enum value the completion-verifier needs is referenced by the framework but never named in the plan's dataset vocabulary):
- [ ] Pin the completion-verifier spec's discrimination axis and name the `expect` values its dataset uses — the plan enumerates the dataset vocabulary only as "finding/pass[/fail]", but completion-verifier is a VERIFIER-style reviewer (it grades a verdict's validity, like plan-review-verifier), and the strict validator's verifier branch REQUIRES both `high_validity` AND `low_validity` when any validity-axis case is present (eval.py:252-254, ALLOWED_EXPECTS={finding,fail,pass,high_validity,low_validity} at eval_scorers.py:32-35). If completion-verifier is meant to discriminate true-vs-false verdicts (the natural design, gated by `discriminates_true_from_false`/`no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` — eval_scorers.py:173-194), its dataset must carry the high_validity/low_validity vocabulary the plan never mentions — an enum value an executor needs but the plan leaves undefined. Conversely, if completion-verifier is a plain fire/no-fire reviewer (only finding/pass), say so and name `emits_valid_verification`/`emits_valid_verdict` as the contract scorer so the executor does not default to the wrong axis. As written the plan under-specifies which of the two scorer/`expect` vocabularies the completion-verifier spec instantiates.
  - criteria: [T8]
  - location: plan "What" bullet 2 ("a balanced corpus-tagged dataset (finding/pass[/fail])"); AC line 1 (completion-verifier.eval.yaml); AC line 3 ("Datasets are balanced"); src/rebar/llm/eval.py:252-254; src/rebar/llm/eval_scorers.py:32-35,173-194; src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:18-29,50-94
  - evidence: The plan's enumerated dataset tags are "finding/pass[/fail]" only — the validity axis (high_validity/low_validity) is ABSENT. But a completion *verifier* is structurally the same archetype as plan-review-verifier, whose dataset is keyed entirely on `expect: high_validity` / `expect: low_validity` paired {true,false} cases (verifier.eval.yaml:55/63/69/77/87/94) and gated by `discriminates_true_from_false` (eval_scorers.py:173). The strict validator enforces the validity axis conditionally (eval.py:252-254: "verifier dataset needs both high_validity and low_validity"), so an executor who builds completion-verifier from the plan's "finding/pass" vocabulary will either (a) build the wrong discrimination axis or (b) trip the strict balance check the plan's own AC#3/#4 rely on. This is the T8 "schema/enum value referenced [by the framework the spec must satisfy] but whose vocabulary is never defined [in the plan]" antipattern, load-bearing for one of the three named specs.
  - suggested_fix: (empty — the author should state whether completion-verifier discriminates verdict validity, e.g. "completion-verifier dataset = paired {met, planted-unmet} verdicts on expect: high_validity / low_validity, gated by discriminates_true_from_false + no_sycophancy_on_real_defects", OR declare it a fire/no-fire verifier on finding/pass with emits_valid_verification; both are defensible, but the plan must name which.)
